title: LMs as Task-Specific Knowledge Bases: An Interpretability Analysis
abstract: arXiv:2606.27237v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Language models (LMs) capture large amounts of factual knowledge applicable to a wide range of tasks, motivating the view of their parameters as a knowledge base. An important property of knowledge bases is that different queries for the same fact return consistent results, drawing on a single source of truth. We investigate whether LMs satisfy this property through behavioral and mechanistic analyses. Our results suggest that they encode knowledge in a task-specific manner. Behaviorally, facts acquired on one task frequently fail to co-emerge on others during training. Parameter localization experiments suggest a mechanistic explanation, revealing distinct parameter subsets underlying different tasks for the same fact. Finally, we show that chain-of-thought reasoning draws part of its effectiveness from engaging task-specific parameters beyond those tied to the evaluation task. Our findings suggest that what the model knows and how it i

translated_title: 语言模型作为任务特定知识库：一项可解释性分析
translated_abstract: 语言模型（LMs）捕获了大量适用于多种任务的事实性知识，这促使人们将其参数视为一种知识库。知识库的一个重要特性是，针对同一事实的不同查询应返回一致的结果，且这些结果源自单一的真实来源。我们通过行为分析和机制分析来探究语言模型是否满足这一特性。结果表明，语言模型以任务特定的方式编码知识。在行为层面，在一个任务上习得的事实通常不会在训练过程中同时出现在其他任务上。参数定位实验提供了一种机制解释，揭示了同一事实在不同任务下对应不同的参数子集。最后，我们展示了思维链推理的部分有效性源于它调用了与评估任务相关的任务特定参数之外的参数。我们的发现表明，模型“知道什么”以及“如何知道”是紧密耦合的。
tldr: 研究发现语言模型以任务特定的方式编码知识，同一事实在不同任务中依赖不同的参数子集，且思维链推理通过调用任务特定参数以外的参数来发挥作用。
en_tdlr: This study reveals that language models encode knowledge in a task-specific manner, with the same fact relying on distinct parameter subsets across tasks, and chain-of-thought reasoning partially draws its effectiveness from engaging parameters beyond those tied to the evaluation task.
